metadata.name: stg_zendesk__audit_log
metadata.comment: The `audit_log` table captures historical changes and actions within Zendesk. It provides a record of modifications  made to tickets, schedules, and other objects, allowing for a detailed audit trail. Each row represents an action  performed by an actor, including the time of the action, the affected entity, and a description of the changes. This  table is especially useful for tracking schedule modifications and maintaining a history of schedule changes.

audit_log_id.name: audit_log_id
audit_log_id.comment: The unique identifier for each audit log entry, representing a distinct action or change.

action.name: action
action.comment: Describes the specific action performed within Zendesk, such as ticket updates or schedule modifications.

actor_id.name: actor_id
actor_id.comment: The unique identifier of the user or system responsible for performing the action.

change_description.name: change_description
change_description.comment: A detailed description of the changes made during the action, capturing what was altered.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The timestamp indicating when the action was performed and recorded in the audit log.

source_id.name: source_id
source_id.comment: The unique identifier of the entity affected by the action, such as a ticket or schedule.

source_label.name: source_label
source_label.comment: A label that provides additional context about the affected entity, typically related to its type or name.

source_type.name: source_type
source_type.comment: Specifies the type of entity impacted by the action, such as a ticket, schedule, or user.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: The timestamp when the record was last synchronized by Fivetran, used to track data freshness.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.comment: Brands are your customer-facing identities. They might represent multiple products or services, or they  might literally be multiple brands owned and represented by your company.

brand_id.name: brand_id
brand_id.comment: The ID automatically assigned when the brand is created

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.comment: If the brand is set as active

source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__daylight_time
metadata.comment: Appropriate offsets (from UTC) for timezones that engage or have engaged with Daylight Savings at some point since 1970.

daylight_end_utc.name: daylight_end_utc
daylight_end_utc.comment: UTC timestamp of when Daylight Time ended in this year.

daylight_offset.name: daylight_offset
daylight_offset.comment: Number of **hours** added during Daylight Savings Time.

daylight_start_utc.name: daylight_start_utc
daylight_start_utc.comment: UTC timestamp of when Daylight Time began in this year.

year.name: year
year.comment: Year in which daylight savings occurred.

daylight_offset_minutes.name: daylight_offset_minutes
daylight_offset_minutes.comment: Number of **minutes** added during Daylight Savings Time.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__group
metadata.comment: When support requests arrive in Zendesk Support, they can be assigned to a Group. Groups serve as the core element of ticket workflow; support agents are organized into Groups and tickets can be assigned to a Group only, or to an assigned agent within a Group. A ticket can never be assigned to an agent without also being  assigned to a Group.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__organization
metadata.comment: Just as agents can be segmented into groups in Zendesk Support, your customers (end-users) can be segmented into  organizations. You can manually assign customers to an organization or automatically assign them to an organization  by their email address domain. Organizations can be used in business rules to route tickets to groups of agents or  to send email notifications.

organization_id.name: organization_id
organization_id.comment: Automatically assigned when the organization is created

details.name: details
details.comment: Any details obout the organization, such as the address

name.name: name
name.comment: A unique name for the organization

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

start_time.name: start_time

time_zone.name: time_zone

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__sla_policy_metric_history
metadata.comment: Tracks historical changes to SLA policy targets at various ticket priority levels.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

sla_policy_id.name: sla_policy_id
sla_policy_id.comment: The ID of the SLA policy associated with this metric.

index.name: index
index.comment: Index number representing priority level within the SLA policy.

sla_policy_updated_at.name: sla_policy_updated_at
sla_policy_updated_at.comment: Timestamp indicating when the SLA policy was last updated, used to track policy changes over time.

valid_starting_at.name: valid_starting_at
valid_starting_at.comment: The time at which the SLA policy metric targets became valid.

valid_ending_at.name: valid_ending_at
valid_ending_at.comment: The time at which the SLA policy metric targets were invalidated.

in_business_hours.name: in_business_hours
in_business_hours.comment: Boolean indicating whether the metric targets are being measured in business hours or calendar hours.

metric.name: metric
metric.comment: The type of SLA metric being tracked. Possible values include "agent_work_time", "first_reply_time", "next_reply_time", "pausable_update_time", "periodic_update_time", "requester_wait_time", and "total_resolution_time".

priority.name: priority
priority.comment: Priority that a ticket must match to be subject to this SLA metric, such as urgent, high, normal, or low.

target.name: target
target.comment: The total time within which the end-state for a metric should be met, measured in minutes

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: The timestamp when the record was last synchronized by Fivetran, used to track data freshness.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__ticket
metadata.comment: Tickets are the means through which your end users (customers) communicate with agents in Zendesk Support. Tickets can  originate from a number of channels, including email, Help Center, chat, phone call, Twitter, Facebook, or the API.

ticket_id.name: ticket_id
ticket_id.comment: Automatically assigned when the ticket is created

_fivetran_deleted.name: _fivetran_deleted
_fivetran_deleted.comment: Boolean created by Fivetran to indicate whether the record has been deleted.

assignee_id.name: assignee_id
assignee_id.comment: The agent currently assigned to the ticket

brand_id.name: brand_id
brand_id.comment: Enterprise only. The id of the brand this ticket is associated with

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: When this record last got updated

description.name: description
description.comment: Read-only first comment on the ticket

due_at.name: due_at
due_at.comment: If this is a ticket of type "task" it has a due date. Due date format uses ISO 8601 format.

group_id.name: group_id
group_id.comment: The group this ticket is assigned to

external_id.name: external_id
external_id.comment: An id you can use to link Zendesk Support tickets to local records.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.comment: Is true if any comments are public, false otherwise

priority.name: priority
priority.comment: The urgency with which the ticket should be addressed, possible values are urgent, high, normal and low

recipient.name: recipient
recipient.comment: The original recipient e-mail address of the ticket

requester_id.name: requester_id
requester_id.comment: The user who requested this ticket

status.name: status
status.comment: The state of the ticket, possible values are new, open, pending, hold, solved and closed

subject.name: subject
subject.comment: The value of the subject field for this ticket

problem_id.name: problem_id
problem_id.comment: For tickets of type "incident", the ID of the problem the incident is linked to.

submitter_id.name: submitter_id
submitter_id.comment: The user who submitted the ticket. The submitter always becomes the author of the first comment on the ticket

ticket_form_id.name: ticket_form_id
ticket_form_id.comment: Enterprise only. The id of the ticket form to render for the ticket

type.name: type
type.comment: The type of this ticket, possible values are problem, incident, question or task

url.name: url
url.comment: The API url of this ticket

created_channel.name: created_channel
created_channel.comment: The channel the ticket was created from

source_from_id.name: source_from_id
source_from_id.comment: The channel the ticket was created from

source_from_title.name: source_from_title
source_from_title.comment: The channel the ticket was created from

source_rel.name: source_rel
source_rel.comment: The rel the ticket was created from

source_to_address.name: source_to_address
source_to_address.comment: The address of the source the ticket was created from

source_to_name.name: source_to_name
source_to_name.comment: The name of the source the ticket was created from

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__ticket_chat
metadata.comment: Information about ticket chat conversations.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: The timestamp when the record was last synchronized by Fivetran, used to track data freshness.

is_authenticated.name: is_authenticated
is_authenticated.comment: Boolean indicating whether the user has been given access to restricted Help Center content and authenticated Chat.

backend.name: backend
backend.comment: Indicates the underlying system or technology that is processing and managing the chat session associated with a ticket.

channel.name: channel
channel.comment: The channel the conversation was created from. Likely `native_messaging` or `chat`.

integration_id.name: integration_id
integration_id.comment: Identifier for the specific integration that created or is associated with the chat ticket.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.comment: ID of the customer user who initiated the chat.

visitor_id.name: visitor_id
visitor_id.comment: Uniquely identify a visitor who is engaged in a chat session. This ID is typically assigned when a visitor starts a chat session on a platform integrated with Zendesk Chat.  It helps track the visitor's interactions across different chat sessions and can be used to re-associate returning visits or sessions with the same visitor.

initiator.name: initiator
initiator.comment: This field is represented as a numeric value and is generally interpreted as: 1 = Agent-initiated (human Zendesk agent started the chat), 2 = End-user-initiated (customer/visitor clicked a web-widget, replied on WhatsApp, Instagram, etc.),  5 = System-initiated (Zendesk’s Flow Builder, Answer Bot, or another automation created the conversation before any human agent stepped in)

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__ticket_chat_event
metadata.comment: Information about chat and messaging events on tickets. Excludes records in which the `actor_id` is either `__trigger` or `__system`.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: The timestamp when the record was last synchronized by Fivetran, used to track data freshness.

actor_id.name: actor_id
actor_id.comment: Zendesk user id for the user or system that triggered the event.

external_message_id.name: external_message_id
external_message_id.comment: External identifier of the chat message.

filename.name: filename
filename.comment: Name of an attached file for `ChatFileAttachment` types of events.

is_history_context.name: is_history_context
is_history_context.comment: Boolean that signifies whether a specific chat event is part of the historical context being constructed within the table. This implies that the event is being recorded as a  part of the historical data collection process, where previous interactions or statuses are captured to provide a complete view of changes over time.

mime_type.name: mime_type
mime_type.comment: MIME type of the document for `ChatFileAttachment` types of events.

original_message_type.name: original_message_type
original_message_type.comment: Type of message. Including but not limited to - `file`, `text`, `form_response`, `form`, and `image`.

parent_message_id.name: parent_message_id
parent_message_id.comment: id of the parent chat message.

reason.name: reason
reason.comment: The underlying cause or justification for a specific event (such as the session ending) within the chat.

size.name: size
size.comment: Size of an attached file for `ChatFileAttachment` types of events.

status.name: status
status.comment: Status of the chat - including but not limited to `READ`, `SEND_SUCCESS`, and `DELIVERY_SUCCESS`. Populated for `ChatMessageStatus` types of events.

status_updated_at.name: status_updated_at
status_updated_at.comment: Timestamp at which the status was updated.

type.name: type
type.comment: Type of chat event. Including but not limited to - `ChatMessage`, `ChatJoin`, `ChatLeave`, `ChatMessageStatus`, `ChatFileAttachment`, `ChatAcdSkillsDetected`, `ChatDepartmentTransfer`, `ChatEmailChanged`, `ChatNameChanged`, `ChatRatingChanged`, and `ChatRatingCommentChanged`.

url.name: url
url.comment: URL of the attached file for `ChatFileAttachment` types of events.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.comment: Ticket comments represent the conversation between requesters, collaborators, and agents. Comments can be public or private.

ticket_comment_id.name: ticket_comment_id
ticket_comment_id.comment: Automatically assigned when the comment is created

_fivetran_deleted.name: _fivetran_deleted
_fivetran_deleted.comment: Boolean created by Fivetran to indicate whether the record has been deleted.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time the comment was created

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.comment: Boolean field indicating if the comment is public (true), or if it is an internal note (false)

ticket_id.name: ticket_id
ticket_id.comment: The ticket id associated with this comment

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.comment: The id of the comment author

is_facebook_comment.name: is_facebook_comment
is_facebook_comment.comment: Boolean field indicating if the comment is a facebook comment

is_tweet.name: is_tweet
is_tweet.comment: Boolean field indicating if the comment is a twitter tweet

is_voice_comment.name: is_voice_comment
is_voice_comment.comment: Boolean field indicating if the comment is a voice comment

source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__ticket_sla_policy

ticket_id.name: ticket_id
ticket_id.comment: The ID of the ticket to which the SLA policy was applied.

sla_policy_id.name: sla_policy_id
sla_policy_id.comment: The ID of the SLA policy that was applied to the ticket.

policy_applied_at.name: policy_applied_at
policy_applied_at.comment: Timestamp indicating when the SLA policy was applied to the ticket.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: The timestamp when the record was last synchronized by Fivetran, used to track data freshness.

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__ticket_tag
metadata.comment: Tags are words, or combinations of words, you can use to add more context to tickets. The table lists all tags currently associated with a ticket.

ticket_id.name: ticket_id
ticket_id.comment: The ID of the ticket associated with the tag

tags.name: tags
tags.comment: The tag, or word(s), associated with the ticket

source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__time_zone
metadata.comment: Offsets (from UTC) for each timezone.

standard_offset.name: standard_offset
standard_offset.comment: Standard offset of the timezone (non-daylight savings hours). In `+/-hh:mm` format.

standard_offset_minutes.name: standard_offset_minutes
standard_offset_minutes.comment: Standard offset of the timezone (non-daylight savings hours) in minutes.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

metadata.name: stg_zendesk__user
metadata.comment: Zendesk Support has three types of users, end-users (your customers), agents, and administrators.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.comment: Automatically assigned when the user is created

external_id.name: external_id
external_id.comment: A unique identifier from another system. The API treats the id as case insensitive.

_fivetran_deleted.name: _fivetran_deleted
_fivetran_deleted.comment: Boolean created by Fivetran to indicate whether the record has been deleted.

last_login_at.name: last_login_at
last_login_at.comment: Last time the user signed in to Zendesk Support or made an API request using an API token or basic authentication.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The time the user was created

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The time the user was last updated.

email.name: email
email.comment: The user's primary email address. *Writeable on create only. On update, a secondary email is added. See Email Address

organization_id.name: organization_id
organization_id.comment: The id of the user's organization. If the user has more than one organization memberships, the id of the user's default organization

role.name: role
role.comment: The user's role. Possible values are "end-user", "agent", or "admin"

ticket_restriction.name: ticket_restriction
ticket_restriction.comment: Specifies which tickets the user has access to. Possible values are organization, groups, assigned, requested and null

time_zone.name: time_zone
time_zone.comment: The user's time zone. See Time Zone

locale.name: locale
locale.comment: The user's locale. A BCP-47 compliant tag for the locale. If both "locale" and "locale_id" are present on create or update, "locale_id" is ignored and only "locale" is used.

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.comment: false if the user has been deleted

is_suspended.name: is_suspended
is_suspended.comment: Boolean representing whether the user has been suspended, meaning that they can no longer sign in and any new support requests you receive from them are sent to the suspended tickets queue.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.comment: Table containing all tags associated with a user. Only present if your account has user tagging enabled.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

metadata.name: zendesk__document
metadata.comment: Each record represents a Zendesk ticket, enriched with data about it's tags, assignees, requester, submitter, organization and group.

chunk_index.name: chunk_index
chunk_index.comment: The index of the chunk associated with the `document_id`.

chunk_tokens_approximate.name: chunk_tokens_approximate
chunk_tokens_approximate.comment: Approximate number of tokens for the chunk, assuming 4 characters per token.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: zendesk__sla_policies
metadata.comment: Each record represents an SLA policy event and additional sla breach and achievement metrics. Calendar and business hour SLA breaches for `first_reply_time`, `next_reply_time`, `requester_wait_time`, and `agent_work_time` are supported. If there is a SLA you would like supported that is not included, please create a feature request.

sla_event_id.name: sla_event_id
sla_event_id.comment: A surrogate key generated from the combination of `ticket_id`, `metric`, `sla_applied_at`, and `source_relation` fields

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

ticket_id.name: ticket_id
ticket_id.comment: A ticket's unique identifier, it is automatically assigned when the ticket is created

sla_policy_name.name: sla_policy_name
sla_policy_name.comment: The name of the SLA policy associated with the SLA metric

metric.name: metric
metric.comment: The SLA metric, either agent_work_time, requester_wait_time, first_reply_time or next_reply_time

sla_applied_at.name: sla_applied_at
sla_applied_at.comment: When the SLA target was triggered. This is the starting time

in_business_hours.name: in_business_hours
in_business_hours.comment: Boolean field indicating if the SLA target is in business hours (true) or calendar hours (false)

priority_applied.name: priority_applied
priority_applied.comment: The ticket priority level that was in effect when the SLA event was applied.

sla_breach_at.name: sla_breach_at
sla_breach_at.comment: The time or expected time of the SLA breach or achieve event.

sla_elapsed_time.name: sla_elapsed_time
sla_elapsed_time.comment: The total elapsed time to achieve the SLA metric whether breached or achieved

is_active_sla.name: is_active_sla
is_active_sla.comment: Boolean field indicating that the SLA event is currently active and not breached (true) or past (false)

is_sla_breach.name: is_sla_breach
is_sla_breach.comment: Boolean field indicating if the SLA has been breached (true) or was achieved (false)

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: zendesk__ticket_backlog
metadata.comment: A daily historical view of the ticket field values defined in the `ticket_field_history_columns` variable  for all backlog tickets. Backlog tickets being defined as any ticket not a 'closed', 'deleted', or 'solved' status.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: The date of the day associated with the field values

ticket_id.name: ticket_id
ticket_id.comment: A ticket's unique identifier, it is automatically assigned when the ticket is created

created_channel.name: created_channel
created_channel.comment: The channel where the ticket was created

assignee_id.name: assignee_id
assignee_id.comment: The agent currently assigned to the ticket

assignee_name.name: assignee_name
assignee_name.comment: The name of the agent currently assigned to the ticket

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

ticket_id.name: ticket_id
ticket_id.comment: Automatically assigned when the ticket is created

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: The timestamp when the record was last synchronized by Fivetran, used to track data freshness.

_fivetran_deleted.name: _fivetran_deleted

assignee_id.comment: The agent currently assigned to the ticket

brand_id.name: brand_id
brand_id.comment: Enterprise only. The id of the brand this ticket is associated with

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: When this record last got updated

description.name: description
description.comment: Read-only first comment on the ticket

due_at.name: due_at
due_at.comment: If this is a ticket of type "task" it has a due date. Due date format uses ISO 8601 format.

group_id.name: group_id
group_id.comment: The group this ticket is assigned to

external_id.name: external_id
external_id.comment: The external id of the ticket

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.comment: Is true if any comments are public, false otherwise

priority.name: priority
priority.comment: The urgency with which the ticket should be addressed, possible values are urgent, high, normal and low

recipient.name: recipient
recipient.comment: The original recipient e-mail address of the ticket

requester_id.name: requester_id
requester_id.comment: The user who requested this ticket

status.name: status
status.comment: The state of the ticket, possible values are new, open, pending, hold, solved and closed

subject.name: subject
subject.comment: The value of the subject field for this ticket

problem_id.name: problem_id
problem_id.comment: The reference to the problem if the ticket is listed as a problem

submitter_id.name: submitter_id
submitter_id.comment: The user who submitted the ticket. The submitter always becomes the author of the first comment on the ticket

ticket_form_id.name: ticket_form_id
ticket_form_id.comment: Enterprise only. The id of the ticket form to render for the ticket

type.name: type
type.comment: The type of this ticket, possible values are problem, incident, question or task

url.name: url
url.comment: The API url of this ticket

created_channel.comment: The channel the ticket was created from

source_from_id.comment: The channel the ticket was created from

source_rel.name: source_rel
source_rel.comment: The rel the ticket was created from

source_to_name.name: source_to_name
source_to_name.comment: The name of the source the ticket was created from

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

is_incident.name: is_incident
is_incident.comment: Boolean indicating whether the ticket is listed as an incident

ticket_brand_name.name: ticket_brand_name
ticket_brand_name.comment: The brand name of with the ticket

ticket_tags.name: ticket_tags
ticket_tags.comment: A list of all tags assigned to this ticket

ticket_form_name.name: ticket_form_name
ticket_form_name.comment: The form name of the ticket

ticket_total_satisfaction_scores.name: ticket_total_satisfaction_scores
ticket_total_satisfaction_scores.comment: The total number of satisfaction scores the ticket received.

ticket_first_satisfaction_score.name: ticket_first_satisfaction_score
ticket_first_satisfaction_score.comment: The first satisfaction score the ticket received.

ticket_satisfaction_score.name: ticket_satisfaction_score
ticket_satisfaction_score.comment: The latest satisfaction score the ticket received.

is_good_to_bad_satisfaction_score.comment: Boolean indicating if the ticket had a satisfaction score went from good to bad.

is_bad_to_good_satisfaction_score.name: is_bad_to_good_satisfaction_score
is_bad_to_good_satisfaction_score.comment: Boolean indicating if the ticket had a satisfaction score went from bad to good.

assignee_locale.comment: The locale of the ticket assignee

assignee_time_zone.name: assignee_time_zone
assignee_time_zone.comment: The time zone of the ticket assignee

assignee_ticket_update_count.name: assignee_ticket_update_count
assignee_ticket_update_count.comment: The number of times the ticket assignee has updated the ticket

assignee_ticket_last_update_at.name: assignee_ticket_last_update_at
assignee_ticket_last_update_at.comment: The last date the ticket assignee updated the ticket

assignee_last_login_at.name: assignee_last_login_at
assignee_last_login_at.comment: The date the ticket assignee last logged in

group_name.name: group_name
group_name.comment: The name of the group this ticket is assigned to

organization_name.name: organization_name
organization_name.comment: The name of the organization of the requester

requester_tag.name: requester_tag
requester_tag.comment: The tags associated with the ticket requester.

submitter_tag.name: submitter_tag
submitter_tag.comment: The tags associated with the ticket submitter.

assignee_tag.name: assignee_tag
assignee_tag.comment: The tags associated with the ticket assignee.

first_reply_time_calendar_minutes.name: first_reply_time_calendar_minutes
first_reply_time_calendar_minutes.comment: The number of calendar minutes between when the ticket was created and when the first public agent response occurred

total_reply_time_calendar_minutes.name: total_reply_time_calendar_minutes
total_reply_time_calendar_minutes.comment: The combined calendar time between all end-user comments and the next public agent response

count_agent_comments.name: count_agent_comments
count_agent_comments.comment: Count of agent comments within the ticket

count_public_agent_comments.name: count_public_agent_comments
count_public_agent_comments.comment: Count of public agent comments within the ticket

count_end_user_comments.name: count_end_user_comments
count_end_user_comments.comment: Count of end user comments within the ticket

count_public_comments.name: count_public_comments
count_public_comments.comment: Count of public comments within the ticket

count_internal_comments.name: count_internal_comments
count_internal_comments.comment: Count of internal comments within the ticket

total_comments.name: total_comments
total_comments.comment: Total count of all comments within the ticket

count_ticket_handoffs.name: count_ticket_handoffs
count_ticket_handoffs.comment: Count of distinct internal users who have touched/commented on the ticket.

ticket_last_comment_date.name: ticket_last_comment_date
ticket_last_comment_date.comment: The time the last comment was applied to the ticket

unique_assignee_count.name: unique_assignee_count
unique_assignee_count.comment: The count of unique assignees that were assigned to the ticket

assignee_stations_count.name: assignee_stations_count
assignee_stations_count.comment: The total number of assignees that were assigned to the ticket

group_stations_count.name: group_stations_count
group_stations_count.comment: The total count of group stations within the ticket

first_assignee_id.name: first_assignee_id
first_assignee_id.comment: Assignee id of the first agent assigned to the ticket

last_assignee_id.name: last_assignee_id
last_assignee_id.comment: Assignee id of the last agent assigned to the ticket

first_agent_assignment_date.name: first_agent_assignment_date
first_agent_assignment_date.comment: The date the first agent was assigned to the ticket

last_agent_assignment_date.name: last_agent_assignment_date
last_agent_assignment_date.comment: The date the last agent was assigned to the ticket

first_solved_at.name: first_solved_at
first_solved_at.comment: The time the ticket was first in 'solved' status

last_solved_at.name: last_solved_at
last_solved_at.comment: The time the ticket was last in 'solved' status

first_assignment_to_resolution_calendar_.name: first_assignment_to_resolution_calendar_minutes
first_assignment_to_resolution_calendar_.comment: The time in calendar minutes between the first assignment and resolution of the ticket

last_assignment_to_resolution_calendar_m.name: last_assignment_to_resolution_calendar_minutes
last_assignment_to_resolution_calendar_m.comment: The time in calendar minutes between the last assignment and resolution of the ticket

ticket_unassigned_duration_calendar_minu.name: ticket_unassigned_duration_calendar_minutes
ticket_unassigned_duration_calendar_minu.comment: The time in minutes the ticket was in an unassigned state

first_resolution_calendar_minutes.name: first_resolution_calendar_minutes
first_resolution_calendar_minutes.comment: The number of calendar minutes between the ticket created time and the time the ticket was first set to solved status

final_resolution_calendar_minutes.name: final_resolution_calendar_minutes
final_resolution_calendar_minutes.comment: The number of calendar minutes between the ticket created time and the time the ticket was last set to solved status

count_reopens.name: count_reopens
count_reopens.comment: The count of ticket reopen events

ticket_deleted_count.name: ticket_deleted_count
ticket_deleted_count.comment: The count of ticket deletion events.

total_ticket_recoveries.name: total_ticket_recoveries
total_ticket_recoveries.comment: The count of ticket recovery (deletion reversal) events.

last_status_assignment_date.name: last_status_assignment_date
last_status_assignment_date.comment: The time the status was last changed on the ticket

new_status_duration_in_calendar_minutes.name: new_status_duration_in_calendar_minutes
new_status_duration_in_calendar_minutes.comment: The duration in calendar minutes the ticket was in the "new" status

open_status_duration_in_calendar_minutes.name: open_status_duration_in_calendar_minutes
open_status_duration_in_calendar_minutes.comment: The duration in calendar minutes the ticket was in the "open" status

agent_wait_time_in_calendar_minutes.name: agent_wait_time_in_calendar_minutes
agent_wait_time_in_calendar_minutes.comment: The combined number of calendar minutes the ticket was in 'pending' status

requester_wait_time_in_calendar_minutes.name: requester_wait_time_in_calendar_minutes
requester_wait_time_in_calendar_minutes.comment: The combined number of calendar minutes the ticket was in 'new', 'open', or 'hold' status

solve_time_in_calendar_minutes.name: solve_time_in_calendar_minutes
solve_time_in_calendar_minutes.comment: The combined number of calendar minutes the ticket was in 'new', 'open', 'hold', or 'pending' status

agent_work_time_in_calendar_minutes.name: agent_work_time_in_calendar_minutes
agent_work_time_in_calendar_minutes.comment: The combined number of calendar minutes the ticket was in 'new' or 'open' status

on_hold_time_in_calendar_minutes.name: on_hold_time_in_calendar_minutes
on_hold_time_in_calendar_minutes.comment: The combined number of calendar minutes the ticket was on 'hold' status

total_agent_replies.name: total_agent_replies
total_agent_replies.comment: The total number of agent replies within the ticket, excluding comments where an agent created the ticket

requester_last_login_age_minutes.name: requester_last_login_age_minutes
requester_last_login_age_minutes.comment: The time in minutes since the ticket requester was last logged in

assignee_last_login_age_minutes.name: assignee_last_login_age_minutes
assignee_last_login_age_minutes.comment: The time in minutes since the ticket assignee was last logged in

unsolved_ticket_age_minutes.name: unsolved_ticket_age_minutes
unsolved_ticket_age_minutes.comment: The time in minutes the ticket has been in an unsolved state

unsolved_ticket_age_since_update_minutes.name: unsolved_ticket_age_since_update_minutes
unsolved_ticket_age_since_update_minutes.comment: The time in minutes the ticket has been unsolved since the last update

is_one_touch_resolution.name: is_one_touch_resolution
is_one_touch_resolution.comment: A boolean field indicating that the ticket has one public agent response and is in solved status

is_two_touch_resolution.name: is_two_touch_resolution
is_two_touch_resolution.comment: Boolean indicating if the ticket was resolved in two public comments

is_multi_touch_resolution.name: is_multi_touch_resolution
is_multi_touch_resolution.comment: Boolean indicating if the ticket was resolved in two or more public comments

first_resolution_business_minutes.name: first_resolution_business_minutes
first_resolution_business_minutes.comment: The number of business minutes between the ticket created time and the time the ticket was first set to solved status

full_resolution_business_minutes.name: full_resolution_business_minutes
full_resolution_business_minutes.comment: The number of business minutes between the ticket created time and the time the ticket was last set to solved status

first_reply_time_business_minutes.name: first_reply_time_business_minutes
first_reply_time_business_minutes.comment: The number of business minutes between when the ticket was created and when the first public agent response occurred

agent_wait_time_in_business_minutes.name: agent_wait_time_in_business_minutes
agent_wait_time_in_business_minutes.comment: The combined number of business minutes the ticket was in 'pending' status

requester_wait_time_in_business_minutes.name: requester_wait_time_in_business_minutes
requester_wait_time_in_business_minutes.comment: The combined number of business minutes the ticket was in 'new', 'open', or 'hold' status

solve_time_in_business_minutes.name: solve_time_in_business_minutes
solve_time_in_business_minutes.comment: The combined number of business minutes the ticket was in 'new', 'open', 'hold', or 'pending' status

agent_work_time_in_business_minutes.name: agent_work_time_in_business_minutes
agent_work_time_in_business_minutes.comment: The combined number of business minutes the ticket was in 'new' or 'open' status

on_hold_time_in_business_minutes.name: on_hold_time_in_business_minutes
on_hold_time_in_business_minutes.comment: The combined number of business minutes the ticket was on 'hold' status

new_status_duration_in_business_minutes.name: new_status_duration_in_business_minutes
new_status_duration_in_business_minutes.comment: The combined number of business minutes the ticket was in 'new' status

open_status_duration_in_business_minutes.name: open_status_duration_in_business_minutes
open_status_duration_in_business_minutes.comment: The combined number of business minutes the ticket was in 'open' status

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: zendesk__ticket_summary
metadata.comment: A single record table containing Zendesk ticket and user summary metrics. These metrics are updated for the current day the model is run.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The schema or database this record came from if you are unioning multiple Zendesk connectors together in this package. Empty string if you are not.

suspended_user_count.name: suspended_user_count
suspended_user_count.comment: Total count of users in a suspended state

new_ticket_count.name: new_ticket_count
new_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets in the "new" status

on_hold_ticket_count.name: on_hold_ticket_count
on_hold_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets in the "hold" status

open_ticket_count.name: open_ticket_count
open_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets in the "open" status

pending_ticket_count.name: pending_ticket_count
pending_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets in the "pending" status

problem_ticket_count.name: problem_ticket_count
problem_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets labeled as problems

reassigned_ticket_count.name: reassigned_ticket_count
reassigned_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets that have been reassigned

reopened_ticket_count.name: reopened_ticket_count
reopened_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets that have been reopened

surveyed_satisfaction_ticket_count.name: surveyed_satisfaction_ticket_count
surveyed_satisfaction_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets that have been surveyed for a satisfaction response

unassigned_unsolved_ticket_count.name: unassigned_unsolved_ticket_count
unassigned_unsolved_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets that are unassigned and unsolved

unreplied_ticket_count.name: unreplied_ticket_count
unreplied_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets that have not had a reply

unreplied_unsolved_ticket_count.name: unreplied_unsolved_ticket_count
unreplied_unsolved_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets that have not had a reply and are unsolved

recovered_ticket_count.name: recovered_ticket_count
recovered_ticket_count.comment: Total count of tickets that were deleted then reopened
